sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The blob is stuck at (56, 27) with G at [[59, 25], [59, 26], [60, 27], [59, 28], [59, 29], [61, 27]] with 8 pixels. The blob is 3 rows above G.
I've been clicking the blob to move it toward G, but it always gets stuck just before reaching G. G seems to move away when the blob gets close.
Let me try a completely different approach. Maybe I need to:
1. Click the ring at a different position to spawn the blob at a different location
2. Or maybe I need to click G directly to "capture" it
3. Or maybe the goal is something entirely different
Let me look at the image again. The ring is at the top, the blob is below it, and G is at the bottom. The trail connects them.
Wait - maybe the goal is to have the blob AND G overlap, or to click G when the blob is adjacent to it. Let me try clicking G when the blob is stuck next to it.
Actually, let me try clicking the G pixels one by one. G has boundary [[59, 25], [59, 26], [60, 27], [59, 28], [59, 29], [61, 27]]. Let me click each of these.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Blob stuck at (56, 27), G at [[59, 25], [59, 26], [60, 27], [59, 28], [59, 29], [61, 27]]
# Let me try clicking each G boundary pixel to see if any eliminates G

seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
g_nodes = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'G']

if g_nodes:
    g = g_nodes[0]
    g_b = g['boundary']
    print(f"G boundary: {g_b}, px={g['pixels']}")
    
    # Click each G boundary pixel
    for p in g_b:
        action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': p[0], 'col': p[1]}])
        
        seg = current_frame.segmentation
        nodes = seg['nodes']
        g_nodes = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'G']
        m_nodes = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'M']
        
        g_px = sum(n['pixels'] for n in g_nodes) if g_nodes else 0
        m_px = sum(n['pixels'] for n in m_nodes) if m_nodes else 0
        print(f"After {p}: G_px={g_px}, M_px={m_px}")
        
        if not g_nodes:
            print("G eliminated!")
            break
        if last_action_result.get('level_completed'):
            print("LEVEL COMPLETED!")
            break

# If still stuck, let me try clicking the white trail pixels between blob and G
if g_nodes:
    print("\nTrying trail pixels...")
    w_nodes = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'w']
    for wn in w_nodes:
        wb = wn['boundary']
        for p in wb:
            action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': p[0], 'col': p[1]}])
            seg = current_frame.segmentation
            nodes = seg['nodes']
            g_nodes = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'G']
            g_px = sum(n['pixels'] for n in g_nodes) if g_nodes else 0
            if g_px == 0:
                print(f"After {p}: G eliminated!")
                break
            if last_action_result.get('level_completed'):
                print("LEVEL COMPLETED!")
                break
        if not g_nodes:
            break
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
